bibliographic_data.title: Origin of indirect optical transitions in few-layer MoS2, WS2 and WSe2
bibliographic_data.abstract: It has been well established that single layer MX2 (M=Mo,W and X=S,Se) are direct gap semiconductors with band edges coinciding at the K point in contrast to their indirect gap multilayer counterparts. In few-layer MX2, there are two valleys along the Γ-K line with similar energy. There is little understanding on which of the two valleys forms the conduction band minimum (CBM) in this thickness regime. We investigate the conduction band valley structure in few-layer MX2 by examining the temperature-dependent shift of indirect exciton PL. Hihgly anisotropic thermal expansion of the lattice and corresponding evolution of the band structure result in distinct peak shift for indirect transitions involving the K and Λ (midpoint along Γ-K) valleys. We identify the origin of the indirect emission and concurrently determine the relative energy of these valleys. Our results show that the two valleys compete in energy in few-layer WSe2.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study investigates the conduction band valley structure in few-layer MX2 by examining the temperature-dependent shift of indirect exciton PL, identifying the origin of the indirect emission and determining the relative energy of the K and Λ valleys.
core_content.key_methodology: Temperature-dependent measurement of indirect exciton photoluminescence (PL) to study the competition between conduction band valleys in few-layer MX2.
outcomes: The CBM is located at the Λ point for bilayer MoS2 and WS2
The CBM is located at the K point for bilayer WSe2
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this study, the conduction band valley structure in few-layer MX2 (MoS2, WS2, and WSe2) was investigated by examining the temperature-dependent shift of indirect exciton photoluminescence (PL). The results show that the two valleys compete in energy in few-layer WSe2. For bilayer MoS2 and WS2, the CBM is located at the Λ point, while for bilayer WSe2, it is at the K point.

nodes.label: Temperature-dependent measurement of indirect exciton PL
